free_text: Institute of Low Energy Nuclear Physics.
Gamma ray production cross sections for the interact-
ions of 14.9 MeV neutrons with C,Al,V,Fe and Nb at 90
Natural metallic Al,V,Fe,Nb samples with 3 cm
diameter and 3 cm high. The sample for
C was natural graphite with similar dimensions.
The purity of the samples was more than 99.9 %
Pulsed fast neutron beam was used.
The time resolution of the whole pulsing system was
about 1 nsec. The averaged pulsed deuteron beam current
was 3-4 muA. Neutron intensity was about 5.e+8 n/sec.
The resolution was 1.85 kev for 1.33 mev.
The detector was at 90 degrees relative to the
axis of the incident neutron beam
A cylindrical liquid scintillator ST-451 and a NE-111
plastic scintillator were used for monitoring neutron
flux.Absolute neutron flux was determined by associated
particle counting with a Si(Au) detector at 90 degrees
-Neutron attenuation in the backing and cooling water
The errors of cross sections were estimated taking
1-determining the counts inthe full energy peak 1-100 %
The probable reaction types and transitions were taken
from level scheme in Table of Isotopes ,Lederer,1978
